summary:
The agent concludes that there is insufficient evidence supporting a strict rule against using internal fixation when less than 10 mm of bone exists at the mental foramen; instead, decisions should rely more on factors like fracture instability and alignment than solely on bone volume. Neural protection is important but cannot justify avoiding all forms of stable fixation based only on minimal remaining bony width.  

Final Answer: Disagree.
